channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 83 replies — 102.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-07-07 03:38 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451705
For added, free bullshit, I have several teeth with severe cavities. Some have broken off and the gums grew back over them. One is basically hollow.

62451718
>>62451403
You could leverage this experience with your parents into being a personal care aid or home health aide for other sick and elderly patients. These jobs often struggle to find local workers and have to hire international workers after proving that they could not find a local to take the job. Even rural areas and full of old sick boomers and its growing.

62451719
>>62451331
Truth you don't want to hear isn't hatred. Learn the difference.

62451728
>>62451705
You're in California, apply for every single possible free service they have and physically go into a government center to get someone to set you up. Just spam apply to absolutely everything and make up Mcdonald's tier work history since they'll almost never call references (even better if you have a friend to lie for you though). Aggressively save every penny when you get a job and you'll be alright. You need to try to force yourself to be as optimistic as possible during the entire process and be comfortable with stretching the truth or coming up with a story about what you were doing, everyone does this.

62451729
>>62451718
Great advice, some of these positions include a dental plan I believe

62451733
>>62451705
Which I just looked up and could cost thousands of dollars. Medical night not cover it either.
>>62451718
The reason no one wants that job is because it sucks. Have you ever changed an adults diapers? I would rather eat buckshot then do that again. Besides, I don't think it pays more than a grocery store cashier anyway.

62451737
>>62451733
You've already done it and have experience in it, honestly this seems like the natural way for you, it's like life has been preparing you to deal with shit, adult shit

62451741
>>62451729
Oh good God, are you serious? I can't do the job anyway.

62451742
>>62451539
People who have worked their whole lives at least have learned HOW to work and have a resume to prove it. But that is really only the bare minimum. There is much more to a fulfilling life, much of which has nothing to do with work or money, but still arises out of self-discipline, skill-development, and emotionally maturity.

62451745
>>62451705
Dentalfag reporting in.
If you post some pics I can tell you which ones could be saved with a crown and which you should have extracted ASAP

62451748
>>62451705
Forgot to add you need to fix your teeth by any means necessary, even if it means ripping that shit out of your head because if you don't it will kill you slowly and painfully by infection. Just aggressively pursue free state services.

62451816
I like these threads, it makes me feel better about my own life. But OP seriously how did you get to this point? You would think alarm bells would be going off in your head at age 25, 30 etc. that you need to get your shit together, for fuck sakes your parents aren't going to live forever.

62451821
>>62451099
It's thievery
He can't admit to crimes nigga
You dense

62451832
>>62451403
Wow shouldn't have cut internet mom lol

62451837
Healthcare or zogbot
Neither a comfy choice, but assuming healthcare, you can start sweeping floors then move up to some sort of auxiliary nurse, nursing assistant, then nurse eventually. You could reach 6 figs, but the path would take a fair amount of humiliation, wiping floors and boomer feces

62451874
>>62451745
This anon is offering something genuinely good for seemingly free.
I'm just noting that

62452257
>>62451063
OP check out this guy sno0ks on YouTube, he has just gone through a similar life experience to you and has vlogged about it: https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=VQsdy_yaQAQ&ra=m

62452288
Go to school
Get work experience
Get a real job
Jesus Christ most people figure this out when they’re 18 years old you god damn retard.

62452842
>>62451745
There's nothing to save, I have two teeth that are broken off at the gum line and the gums have grown over them, another tooth lost the back half above the gum line and my lower right molar is broken on top and has a chunk missing.
They all have to come out.

62452847
>>62452288
What school? I'm already a high school graduate.
>>62451821
Food stamps, dum dum.

62453356
What about trying to start a business? Self employment? Why do my only options have to be depressing bullshit?

62453476
>>62453356

62453498
>>62453356
Both are better (best?) options for wealth if you don't plan on going to college and also marrying someone who went to school. Both preferably masters degrees or higher.

62453510
>>62449961
I have an online friend who was a loser NEET for like 10 years, he's still a loser but he started editing videos for youtube channels for a % of revenue and one of them popped off and he's made around 220k in 2 years. If you are autistic about something and use the internet, it's possible, though you have to get lucky and work hard still

62453537
>>62453356
You need some capital to get started, whatever biz you want to open. And since you sound incredibly lazy and your body most likely haven't a single gram of muscular mass, you probably wont do anything outside, correct?
Then you need to make cash from your computer.

I started scalping and flipping TCGs back in 2016 with $4K invested. Slow start but it eventually paid off after a bunch of smart picks on my end (no sealed, i focused on singles at release i thought could become staples, extremely risky and you need to be very good at the game itself to correctly identify unnoticed bangers). Once i secured more cash i could start to focus on vintage staples and that's where the $$ are. Cashed out 100K total so it turned out pretty nicely.

If you have zero you can try to make AI pornslop, twitch content or stuff like that. But starting from scratches, with zero visibility, zero social skill and no determination in a field ultra-saturated with mega grinders who want to succeed... i don't think you'll go very far. Not to discourage you, but what you have showed us of your personality so far tells me you most likely will end up as a beggar living under a bridge.

62453557
>>62453510
Video editing is a shitload of work hours, if you reads OP shitpost you'll quickly understand it's out of his reach. He's basically asking if we can give him a winning lottery ticket, and when we tell him it doesn't work that way, he whines.

62453686
>>62453557
You would need a computer for that and an Internet connection. The desktop unused to have died several years ago and I'm only allowed to use the wifi with my phone.
I would have to move out completely first.

62453688
Also, if you guys could calm down on the personal attacks? I didn't do anything to deserve that shit. Whatever boggeyman lives in your head isn't me.

62453723
God, it feels like I'm having panic attack after panic attack.

62453813
>>62453723
honest to god you should go work on a boat/ship, get paid to roam the seas and hear stuff on the radio and sleep in the middle of the darkness of the ocean and occasionally see whales.

item.title: Index funds are dead — 41 replies — 53.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Index funds are dead

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-07-07 03:40 UTC

OP

Something like 70% of the American public has their money in the stock market.

Index fund investors (“Bogleheads bro!”) want you to believe that they’re all going to be rich because they put $500 a month into the market. This would essentially mean every single American would become rich just by DCAing into the US stock market.

Index investing is dead. The ONLY reason it worked in the past is because like 5% of Americans owned stocks. The ones that got in first benefited from everyone else piling in, exactly like Bitcoin.

Look at Bitcoin, all the Bitcoin retards made the same mistake of telling people it’ll just go up and up forever, now we’ve plateaued for the last 5 years and the Bitcoin trade is dead.

The only winners going forward will be good active investors. You’ve been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62448308
It's all going to crash in the next 5 years, and economic pain will be unlike anything we've ever felt. So enjoy your gay stocks you fucking wealthsimple zoomer nigger.

62448320
>>62448306
It's so over for America saar! India superpower by 2030!

62448334
>>62448306
You're forgetting that since the 1970s and the birth of the Internet we've seen basically all major corporations become multi-national entities, access to the American markets overseas increasing, sovereign wealth funds worth trillions of dollars coming up with holding timelines that basically go to infinity since they're not constrained to the lifespan of a single person, etc.
The days of the USA markets being constrained to just America is fucking over. That's why the Buffett Indicator is such a worthless metric now. The USA has basically conquered the globe in terms of stocks, sure you'll get some thirdies teeth gnashing over this and crying about BRICS but fundamentally the Boglechads are still right and are still early.
>B-BUT THAT MEANS AMERICANS WITH STOCKS ARE GOING TO BE WEALTHY
Yeah, no shit. We're the best country on earth, of course we're wealthy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448451
>>62448407
I'll accept your concession :)

62448604
Fixed mortgages are the reason these hyperinflation dollar is worthless scenarios are such a pipe dream.

62448645
Cool story bro, still buying $250/week though

62448657
>>62448306
>Index fund investors (“Bogleheads bro!”) want you to believe that they’re all going to be rich because they put $500 a month into the market.

We can wiggle with the definition of rich but if you start young yes you'll have millions of dollars by retirement. If you consider people who are actually making decent money and able to put away $1000, $5000, $10k a month it's undeniable.

>This would essentially mean every single American would become rich just by DCAing into the US stock market.

Yes.

>Index investing is dead.

Why is it dead?

>now we’ve plateaued for the last 5 years and the Bitcoin trade is dead.

You sound like a pretty tarded zoomer. If a market ever drops to a 5 year low then it's over? What the fuck is a recession or depression to you?

62448707
>>62448306
>the tulip market failed this proves that index funds are dead
So how's the weather in Karachi, Mahboub?

62448715
Quitting my job was a mistake. It wasn't a good job, but getting yourself hyped up to apply for and get a new job and commute to work is a pain in the ass. It's easier to stick with routine. If I joined the military in 2017 after working at the ice cream shop due to my worthless degree I could have slipped into some meme clearance job working with Servicenow or something for the last 3 years once they stopped caring about vaccines. In 2022 and 2023 I could have just been on unemployment, severance pay, going back to school with military help.

62448739
>>62448715
For a loser the military is a great opportunity to acquire wealth

62448751
>>62448739
Kelly's Heroes was fictional though and I'm tired of pretending it wasn't.

62448752
>>62448339
>taking market risk just to break even

He does it for free!

62449026
>>62448339
It's 7% a year in the future but 5% a year in reality adjusted for inflation

62449374
>>62448308
>t. Doomer from 5 years ago

62449868
So whats better?

62449870
After inflation and taxes, you get like 1-2% from index funds

Its fucking retarded

62449878
>>62448306
>The only winners going forward will be good active investors.

So basically just trading firms with proprietary digital superintelligence who farm everyone else for exit liquidity.

Is it possible, is it just possible, that the average person is screwed, just like they were for 99.999999% of history?

62449882
>>62448306
I was actively stock picking for the past year and I did beat the market but I’m too lazy to constantly be researching and listening to my cabal of financial pundits everyday.

I’m just gonna DCA my paycheck into VOO and a bit of VXUS and let it be. I’m not willing to work harder or hustle. So if this doesn’t work that’s too bad Sonny

62449891
the only way to beat the market is buying bitcoin or leveraged bitcoin at the bottom, the rest is gambling

62449899
>>62449870
>taxes

Found the dumb incel crybaby who hasn't read the tax code

62450040
>>62448306
>70% of Americans have money in index funds
>index funds are dead
"No one goes there anymore, it's too crowded"

62450136
The real reason index funds are dying is because bailing out and enshrining the monopolies of the top companies in every index is no longer and will no longer be a popular investment strategy when Israel wields trump over Iran having some uranium and there won't be an occupy wall Street idpol smokescreen to bail out the AI bubble popping that is only what it is because of east Asian ram fan plants being extremely mafioso with how they deal to the mag7 who only survive by perpetuating the myth that the minimum wage rising is good for the economy and not a root cause of evaporating the middle class with inflation. If you get laid off because of ai you are not investing in an AI bubble boondoggle index fund that is 20%+ Nvidia, msft, AAPL, goog, meta, pltr, intc etc.... you are investing bear

62450142
I'm gonna get so fucking rich from my investments, I'm so lucky money wise.

62450144
>>62450142
You eatin good soon bro, donchu worry bout it :)

62450343
>>62448334
Holy shit that literal 30 year crab market from 1970 to 2000.

That couldn't happen again... right bros..?

The whole "10% average return per year over the last 100 years" thing wasn't calculated by taking into account the massive asset bubble of the last 5 years... was it bros...?

62450420
>>62448366
shut the fuck up you dumb pajeet

62450553
>>62450343
You didn't actually read the caption of the chart did you, speed-reader-kun?

62450666
>>62448334
> We're the best country on earth, of course we're wealthy.

62450677
>>62448752
literally anything else carries exponentially higher risk.

>>62449026
if you believe the government figure for inflation you really are the best goy

62450682
>>62449870
LTCG ensures if you only pull out 120k a year (in profit for married couples only) you'll pay no federal tax.

62450894
>>62450677
I don't

62451585
>>62448715
>It wasn't a good job, but getting yourself hyped up to apply for and get a new job and commute to work is a pain in the ass.

Very relatable, anon. I am 36 and have come to this conclusion as well. I am content to stay put at a place that is tolerable, even if I could make more somewhere else. Can't handle the stress anymore. Done with moving, commuting, and the interview process if I have anything to say about it. (Tradie who took a paycut for a low stress maintenance gig vs high paying construction money.)

62453823
there is not a single possible future scenario where normies are collectively allowed to retire early. it is a mathematical impossibility.

item.title: Would you eat a baby for 30000 LINK? — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Would you eat a baby for 30000 LINK?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-07 04:24 UTC

OP

Would you eat a baby for 30000 LINK?

Early replies (first 3)

62452053
>>62452048
Most Linkies would unironically do that because they're deranged cuckolds who worship a Saturnian/Jewish token (even if they never got rich off it)

I wouldn't though because my sould is worth much more than some premined shitcoin

62452190
>>62452048
What color is the baby?

62452215
>>62452048
I dont want AIDS

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62452247
>>62452048
I would do it for free.

62452338
30000 link is gonna be like four cents soon so no

62452476
>>62452053
Or is it because it would make your IBS flare up? LMAO

62452505
>>62452476
Kek

62452606
>>62452053
I use literally to harden and anchor the actual objectivity of what I write to punctuate each poinit to remind faggots like you not everyone is just gushing team sports slop and there is an actual reality despite your persistent, deep attempt to repress it in yourself and repress its expression to others. This entire schtick is so fucking stupid and low effort/IQ. "Oooooh I don't like that you proved me wrong so you're a russian bot oh and did you know Trump said they were very fine people!!!!!" It is literally indistinguishable from this level of rhetoric. Why would I rewrite the same information multiple times which you are unable to engage with and spend valuable time of my life redundantly generating the same framed concept when I can just reshare it? Lol You spend hours sperging and never comment on ANYTHING substantial claimed and call me effeminate but literally just do lefty hivemind power messaging tactics to try to save face in arguments you admit to yourself and everyone you can't win. The irony is in all of your accusation you are literally an NPC in human bot form, and despite this all, the same way you tacitly admit that you know your position is indefensible by being this way, you also admit you recognize our argumentative supremacy over you by (probably totally unknown to you) absorbing specific terminology used to try to superficially emulate us through osmosis thinking using these words will transfer the value of what we say when the whole point is that we are right on substance and you have nothing to say and don't care what's true and it doesn't matter which words you use to express this. REALLY fucking gay way to live and be and if you are a paid poster, which is very possible, you are a spineless plague rat of jews with negative value add into the world and a shame to your ancestors

62453083
>>62452048
No.

62453931
>>62452048
Am I allowed to sell the link instantly upon receiving it (I still wouldn't do it, but this is the worst possible incentive to, though I imagine most linkie cultists would).

62453933
>>62452606
t. seething linkie copypaste [can't] copefag

item.title: IT'S OFFICIAL!!!!! Ripple has received its EU CASP license. We are now fully MiCA-compliant and ready to meet growing Eu… — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT'S OFFICIAL!!!!! Ripple has received its EU CASP license. We are now fully MiCA-compliant and ready to meet growing Eu…

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-07 04:25 UTC

OP

IT'S OFFICIAL!!!!!

Ripple has received its EU CASP license. We are now fully MiCA-compliant and ready to meet growing European crypto demand!

>https://x.com/Ripple/status/2074048653019279478

Early replies (first 3)

62452299
>$1.15
So fucking bullish

62452323
>>62452224
BEEEEEEEEEUUUUUUUUUMMM

62452333
>>62452224
>xrp
>demand
lmao
also price, chart

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62452437
>>62452224
So, two more weeks?

62452438
>>62452224
BOOM status?

62452444
>>62452224
As a European, I assure you that nobody here cares—to an extent you can't even imagine.

62452457
>>62452444
It’s not for you dumbass. Institutions want regulation and you’ll get it through your new EUDI digital wallet because of them

62452467
>>62452438
Ka

62452469
Crypto is so fucking dead

62453940
>>62452224
Based as fuck! Ripple are the (non-evil) Microsoft of web3.

item.title: Day 354 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $10 — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 354 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $10

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-07 04:22 UTC

OP

Day 354 of snailposting every day until LINK is at $10

Early replies (first 3)

62451040
>>62451028
baaaaased and cuckold pilled

True marines are willing to hold for another 10 years. We always knew it was a twenty year hodl.

PRAISE KEK
77777

62451048
>We always knew it was a FOREVER AND EVER hodl.

nobody is getting you old outdated crap bags!

62451566
>>62451040
>>62451048
fuddies working OT today

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451577
>>62451566
Based and praise kek my fellow Link marines. Those dirty fuddies want to steal your precious Link tokens, don't fall for their FUD.

Praise kek
shadilay!

62451649
>>62451577
nobody wants you shitlink

62451937
>>62451028
Soon we will be flapping on the wings of a butterfly! Or is that a caterpillar? I forget (just kidding)! Someone call Bill Nye the Based Science Guy to help sort this out! Actually we probably don't need to call him because he is for sure a linkie, he's SMART!!!!!!! GO GO GADGET LINK INVESTMENT!!!!

62451988
>>62451937
repeat after me

we will NEVER sell
we are warriors
we are Link marines

62452046
>>62451988

We will NEVER sell
We are warriors
WE ARE LINK MARINES!!!!!!!!!!!!

HOORAH!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

62452574
>>62452046
Gosh my penis is leaking pre cum just looking at Chainlink's chart. I feel such a submissive sissy for Sergey but not in a homo way if you get what I mean

62453927
>>62452574
I am bi personally but I hope our investment works out!

item.title: /biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! — 9 replies — 20.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/biz/ 8 years ago >BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X!

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-07-07 04:00 UTC

OP

/biz/ 8 years ago
>BRO BUY THE DIP ON THIS SPECULATIVE COIN, IT'S GOING 100X! YOU JUST NEED 1K DOLLARS
/biz/ 5 years ago
>OMG BRO SOMEONE THREW 3500 BUCKS/10 ETH INTO THIS COIN, WE HAVE WHALES! I'M UP 400K TODAY!
/biz/ now
>You should NEVER do calls, options, invest in any kind of crypto no matter the bull case, and if a stock is going up a lot you should be afraid because it will crash no matter what. Instead you should start a business- a real one, like contracting or landscaping, not an ecommerce business- and put your spare paychecks into VOO for 40 years.

Where did these people come from? The board is fucking dead but did we get an influx of unironic retired boomers giving out advice that 'worked' in 1966 and is now totally fucking worthless?

Early replies (first 3)

62453596
>>62453585
we've always had 3 or 4 boomers here. But now everyone else left.

coincidentally all those other "people" left when they made the capcha harder and made ban evasion more difficult.

62453601
>>62453585
Jannies killed the board. You cant share alpha or gambles or anything here now.

62453607
cant share any cryptos that arent in the top 100 coins lmao. the 2024/2025 janny (if theyre still here) turned this place into bag holder general

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453619
>You should NEVER do calls, options

True, why do broke retards think they can beat rich smart people

62453628
The start a business fags are just trolling usually from what I see, provide no legit advice.
Invest in VOO fags should be banned just for being so boring. Why even bother to post this. Yes everyone knows put all your money in sp500 for 50 years you will make a few millions. Discussion is for how to beat this.

62453703
>>62453585
Its because making it in crypto is over, the newfag business retards and stock retards took over.
>>62453628
This, people here are retarded and think buying a broad stock index fund is some amazing genius plan but its the bare minimum to seperate yourself from a NPC.

62453842
Everything you posted was nonsense except the part about VOO

62453878
>>62453585
Circumstances have changed, I’m unironically smiling at my VOO and AIQ gains yet back then I was buying options and cryptos. All that money is gone now. I’m 30 now I need to think about my future seriously.
>>62453842
Based. We are expecting the 50% pullback and I will hold it like Charlie Munger says you ought to.

62453881
>>62453585
Its a glow psyop now.

item.title: Which one of you sold 2.1% of her token today for $1,300 bux? — 15 replies — 13.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Which one of you sold 2.1% of her token today for $1,300 bux?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-07 03:18 UTC

OP

Which one of you sold 2.1% of her token today for $1,300 bux?

How can you sell this old White girl, you should be ashamed. Take a good hard look in the mirror right NOW!

(I am not home atm, so at least I won’t be shoah’d from 4chan again for this thread for another week on my home internet)

Early replies (first 3)

62444972
>>62444945
i love she

62445037
>>62444945
She wouldn't lick the peanutbutter of my balls so she had to go, I'm sorry.

62445045
>>62445037
the comments matches the color id

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445079
i used to have a west highland terrior. she was a good dog, but loved rolling around in other dog's shit. very annoying.

62445088
>>62445079
west highland terriers are predators, they will roll in shit or remains of dead animals to hide their scent like little rambo's

62446550
>>62444945
Make a TG faggot.

62446560
I will be honest. received an airdrop. Made me 20k. Ever since, I bought again. Do you think I care about such pocket change? Or do you think I understand how to winmax you retards? And next time that guy s around forewarn me.

62446579
>>62446560
Holy esl your skin is brown

62447209
>>62446550
these girls singing these songs making fun of crypto chuds marked the bottom last cycle btw

62448318
>>62444945
I'm not buying this shit but I'm glad she's still around

62449657
>>62448318
Lily respects ur decision

62449671
>>62446560
I couldn’t decipher most of what you said but since you seemingly dgaf about that $20k pocket change, give me the $12k back that I lost to this shit

62449683
>>62449671
its relaunched on pf now and her coin is at 50k mc or so

you could get that money u lost on eth lily, plus make 10-100-1000X more from getting in on lilybux on pf now at these levels

you do know it was relaunched 6 months ago right? but properly this time, no airdrop autism or any weird bs with apu people

62450364
>>62449683
>launched 6 months ago
>50k mcap
I’m not buying your bags. Scam me once, shame on you. Scam me twice, you can’t get scammed again, retard

62453750
>>62450364
You've been warned

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 6 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-07 03:53 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453805
>>62453622

>you want six figures? Power wash 100 driveways for $100 each, there’s your six figures

62453846
>>62453799
Which came first, the poor or the rich?

62453864
>>62453846
Adam was rich. Then Eve the poor floozy showed up.

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 17 replies — 7.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-07 02:06 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450401
>>62450377

You really wanna know? For most people it’s because owning a house forces you to save via the mortgage payments. Yes, you could put that money into index funds instead or whatever and come out ahead, but normies wouldn’t do that, they’d spend it. For a single /biz/tard buying a house is probably not a good investment. But for most people, especially families, owning a house will improve their financial position over time because they will be forced to build equity over time instead of spending that money on stuff.

That’s why normies say housing is a “good investment”. For them it’s better than the likely alternative.

62450434
>>62450381
>double
you could've just bought MU, SNDK, etc.

62450436
It can work out cheaper than renting, depends on prices, now is probably a bad time to buy. No doubt all kinds of privilege taxes will be heaped on home owners too in the coming years, I think it's smarter to buy property outside of the west, the benefit also is that you can leave to live somewhere else at any time

62450445
Great, another rentoid cope thread

62450446
>>62450377
It’s hard to lose money from owning a house. What are the alternatives? Either rent where you get no money back, or if you’re lucky enough to live with parents who don’t ask for anything. Tax is a non issue. One year of tax is the equivalent of a renter paying one months rent. Maintenance is virtually cost free. Cleaning up the house is what any responsible human being does regardless of whether they own a house or not. Repairs happen anywhere. Learn to DIY. You shouldn’t need to spend $400 for someone to replace a $40 faucet that takes 30 minutes to do. It’s not only about investing from a financial point of view either. Owning a home is more piece of mind and being able to do whatever you want (not counting HOAs). That alone is worth it more than anything else

62450464
Renting when old will be a massive issue in years to come.
Pensions will not cover rent AND living for the vast majority of people.
It's an issue not reared its head yet because boomers own homes and have pensions that cover their living, or they are selling their homes for care.
Unless you know you are going to be wealthy, you need to own a home at some point or be utterly doomed to hardship in an already strained system in your old age.

>inb4 i'll just rope when old LULZ
Childish nonsense talk.

62451291
>>62450377
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
all of this is wrapped up in your rent payment dumbass. you really think those costs don’t exist for your landlord and he’s not passing them on to you, plus profit?

62451450
buying a house cured my depression
best investment ever

62452006
when the market crashes everyone will still own their homes and will have enjoyed living in them but you'll own nothing and will have been living in an apartment listening to blacks keep you awake all night all that time

62452049
>>62450401
yea this tracks. it's like how a 401k is "good" in that it keeps retard normies from spending that chunk of their paycheck on coke and sportsball tickets

a good rule of thumb

62453524
>>62450377
I bought a 3-bedroom and rent out two of the bedrooms. My tenants pay my mortgage for me.
Housing was an "investment" for the last half-century or so, which is a big part of why things are so fucked now in the housing market.

Also, you're already paying for all that shit when you pay rent. You can either pay your rent to a bank directly or pay it to some retard who is skimming off the top. This realization is what led me to buy a house eventually.

62453548
An asset that can bring rent and only goes up in price. There is literally NO comparable investment. The only people crying about it are poor renters.

62453561
>>62450377
I don't like landlords telling me what I can and can't do with their property. I don't like having neighbors upstairs or downstairs. I don't like having to ask if I want something remodeled or changed. I don't like fighting with my neighbors for parking or who gets to use the yard or the laundry room.

also my house costs 1/5 what rent does because I've had it for 25 years and my payment never goes up while your rent has gone up 600% in the same time period.

62453579
>>62450377
You have to pay all that if you rent, except repair for damage that you didn't cause.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 61 replies — 50.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 61 • Updated: 2026-07-07 03:13 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450613
>>62450251
Leave it to the goyim to exchange literal gold for literal paper and think they're getting one up on you.

62450617
>>62450613
Only morons think gold us a good investment

62450619
>>62449388
stock prices have nothing to do with how profitable a company is

62450628
>>62450251
Brown poojeet IQ in display. Receipts exist.

62451091
>>62450617
good goy

62451501
>>62451091
Ngl you should port only a fraction of your wealth in boomer rocks. There’s always a small chance asteroid mining isn’t a meme and boomer rocks does a -99% overnight.

62451725
jugaad

62451765
>>62449441
shut the fuck up jeet, you aren't even potty trained plus your people actually eat cow shit

62451803
>>62450251
Can't return gold.
BUT if you buy it online and don't sign for it twice it automatically goes back to Costco and you get a refund (source, happened to me).
So you could use this for speculation on gold- if you buy it and the price drops before you receive it just don't sign for it.

62451853
>>62451765
You are jelly of our success

62451899
>>62451853
Cope

62451948
>>62451899
Indians are winning

62452213
>>62451948
Seethe

62452229
>>62449388
Why would this be bearish? It increased their DEI score and that means they get infinite free loans from blackrock

62452396
>>62449441
Karen means victory

62452975
>>62452396
Cope

62453019
>>62449388
Indian mentality is completely toxic to civilization

62453036
>/biz/
>fundamentally misunderstanding how Costco works as a business
You could return every single thing you bought at Costco and they'd still be profitable. The entire point of the company is raising capital. They have 60 days to process and refund their distributors for the returns. In that period, the millions they receive in up-front purchases generates them more in investment value with than what all the returns would pay out for. The fact that /biz/ fails to see this is why all of you are poor wagie shitcoin scammers. The business model Costco operates on is highly faceted and multilayered. They make millions a month in renting warehouse shipping palette space alone. The model they use is literally refund-proof and they don't give a shit about you bringing stuff back. Look at their stock chart if you want proof.

62453062
>>62453036
>they can afford it so it's ok to steal
Indian jugaad psychology on display. When every citizen in a country is aligned with this philosophy, it fails.
When there is high trust, there is communal wealth.

62453196
No honour system can survive contact with Jeets

62453215
>>62451853
> list falls off hard and immediately into literally who territory
saaar why is the indian tech industry a joke that is being cannibalize by chatgpt saaar

62453293
>>62453196
>my country is being invaded reeeeee
>better cry on an incel blog 24/7/365 until i die

62453303
>>62451853
>You are jelly of our success
lmao

62453347
>>62453303
You have 20 posts seething about indians who are the richest immigrants in America averaging $200k per year, yes you are jealous of us

62453353
>>62453347
>richest immigrants in America averaging $200k per year
Brapmeet thinks we don't know about his household income stats with ten jeets crammed into each domicile. KEK

62453370
>>62453353
Ten indian Google coders at $400,000 per year each stuffed in one house, in 5 years each one buys a $1 million house for cash.

While you seethe and cry on incel blogs that you deserve everything for free because you are white, lmao no wonder you are bitter

62453683
>>62449441
>Lives in a white country instead of his shithole
Go home Rajesh nobody likes you

62453692
>>62453683
Your master invited me in because you are dumb and lazy and im smart and hardworking

62453742
>>62453062
This is the most blatant
>Thing: Japan
I've ever seen in my life. As if all of rural north america doesn't do this as well.

item.content:encoded:

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442503
>>62442227

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228

62442553
>>62442511

62442563

62442650

62442658

62442668

62443383

62443384

Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018

62445689

62446816

62447133

62447140

62447522

62448616
>>62426537

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375

62450895

62450912
>>62426512

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 140 replies — 21.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 140 • Updated: 2026-07-06 21:58 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445941
>>62445705
A silly shilly
Being a seethy weethy
Built for GME

62446010
>>62445859
I'm going to frame the picture of Jake on the couch and put it up in my mansion. When guests will ask who he is, I'll smirk and say "you wouldn't get it."

62446088
>>62445569

62446106
>>62445576
>>62445600
>>62445645

spot on

62446149

62446238

62446242

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

62446288

62446344

Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386

the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

62447659
Ryan Cohen confirmed AGAIN that bathroom gods have won by posting on X that shows he has ties to the Icahn family. Shills will immediately come out of the woodwork now that I bring this up. It's over shills, we won. It's been 100% confirmed.

62447686
>>62447659
it's not brett icahn's fiance the birthmarks don't match

62447756
Baggie general

62447918
>>62447756

62448131
lamestop holders are seething because they missed out on the true moass. bathroom gods are the chosen ones. they trusted the plan above all. the cancellation of shares was the ultimate IQ test. weaker people couldn't handle that. now they are filled with regret.

62448146
>>62448131
The silo around BBBY was truly impressive. Superstonk made no mention of it. /GME/ was a full blown silo as well. It wasn't until I looked at the option chain on BBBY and saw the insane volatility that made me look in to it further. Greatest decision of my life.

62449186
>>62449050
all of them are short bbbyq. i guarantee it

62449344
>>62449050
>>62449186
If you are assassinated, does your short position get closed? Asking for Brian Thompson

62449448
>>62449344
Nope. His estate would get liquidated to pay off to buy back the shares.

62450370
CUSIP-6

62450461
>>62448146
The silo around eBay has become palpable

62452261
she has high res photos on her facebook. you can compare the birthmarks on her arm. the redditors are stupid as fuck it's demoralizing.

62452270
it seems that sultan didn't tweet about teddy like i thought he did. fuck's sake.

62452287
>>62452270
Its right here can you stop whining like a fatherless attention starved faggot? Youre scaring the hoes.

https://x.com/Almaadeed/status/2072817361862717939

62452325
>>62452287
ok good it displays the gamestop.com preview on the twitter page but the link he posted is to teddy.com which redirects to gamestop.com

>>62450461

item.title: Index dogs justify your life — 29 replies — 14.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Index dogs justify your life

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-07 02:28 UTC

OP

Adjusted for inflation, half of the decades the S&P 500 yields ZERO return.
You could buy the S&P 500 each month for the next 20 years, and it wouldn't be out of the ordinary if at the end of these 20 years you would have had ZERO returns - adjusted for inflation.

Early replies (first 3)

62451667
>>62451639
So i should exit now and get back in in 2040?

62451701
>>62451639
I mean, it's still worthwhile as an inflation hedge and therefore the base of your portfolio. But yeah, an index fund won't make you rich. It just allows you to save your money. The only way to make money is by picking and trading individual stocks, which is beyond what most people can do while maintaining their full time job.

62451785
>voofaggots convinced me not to buy ai stocks
>this was 8 months ago and now they're up 800%
Never trust voogroids.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451805
>>62451639
>years arbitrarily chosen to capture major stock market depressions
>neglects to mention that the point of dca is to keep buying at the lows as well
You're retarded if you fall for this obvious demoralization.
Keep gambling on memes and stay poor forever, niggers.

62451807
>>62451639
Wow when things are good they’re good and when they’re bad they’re basically just 0 and not negative
This is so bad, you should’ve known what to short during 08 instead or perfectly hedged against the dot com bubble in another way baka

62451824
>>62451639
0 stress
Guaranteed line go up
Just dollar cost average in over time
Beat 95% of hedge funds
It's literally the best move for 99% of humans.

62451829
yes a bear market happens once a decade
yes we are probably due for one
but I'll continue to participate. it could go up 30% from here before going down 20% in a bear correction.

62451922
>>62451701
Thats retarded tho, these companies are getting a free ride basically. You should refuse to index invest on principle because these incompetent fucks cant make money

62451938
>>62451922
I roundtripped the gold run up, got 60-80% gains on the recent chip bull and had the presence of mind to sell.

Then I was feeling frisky and took a small position in 2xDRAM etf and got smoked down 30%. said fuck this and put everything in VOO. I’m done researching everyday and envisioning my theses playing out and adjusting fuck this shit

62452140
>>62451639
The truth of the matter is, most index investors are retards. They’re too stupid to buy AMD, NVDA, MU, and SNDK. They won’t even buy SPCX which will be the first $50T company in history

62452156
>>62451829
>>62451639
wHaT dO YoU mEAN StOCKs cAN go DOWN???

Holy shit zoomers are tard maxed. This is why the traditional advice has been to gradually increase exposure to bonds as one approaches retirement age.

"Maaaan that means I gotta save money and shit"

Yeah, the wages of sin is death, if you don't work you die, etc etc

62452157
>>62452140
Yeah index investors don’t buy those they just buy an index that includes those
Hope this helps!

62452218
>>62451824
I dont think you do beat 95% of hedgefunds

62452222
>>62452157
stay poor!
Hope this helps

62452252
>>62451805
>dca
So you're telling me I should DCA something that was invented 10 years ago when everything was blooming, and I should do it for the next 15 years when everything will be dumping?

62452275
>>62452156
are you well? you sound schizophrenic

62452862
>>62451824
>>62452218
>100x my money in 40 years isn't enough!
Okay jew boomer. 100xing my money from age 20 to 60 is enough for me.

62452948
>>62452222
lol mad mad mad faggot

62452950
>>62452252
He clearly said you should stay poor

62453113
>>62452862
But youre not 100x it...

62453307
>>62451639
>Adjusted for inflation
You are aware of course that inflation hits all humans right? Your $1 in a top tier hedge fund or managed fund is the same as your $1 in an index.

But my whole argument is just what Bogle said:
1. 99% of all financial advisors, hedge funds, managed funds, ect will fail to beat the index after 15 years once fees are taken into account, so picking one of these over an index is illogical
2. Picking the 1% of fund managers who DO beat the market is as improbable as picking the right stock & picking the 1% fund manager typically requires far more capital or special access than 99% of people can afford
3. Of all the places you could park money, indexes are low cost and high returning and will beat inflation if its the right country
4. The right country at the time of his book and analysis was the USA, and arguably still is today, due to it being the global military power (safe from invasion), the most capitalist promoting country on the planet and one of the most advanced economies for goods and service development. China is better, but not true capitalism so we cant extract its value.
5. The only other ways to get wealthy are to start a business (3% return a profit on average in the West) or stock picking, which the average person cannot do with any consistency over 40+ years of a life combined with fees and taxes.

I followed his advice, and to my hubris did stock picking anyway. I have returned 14.5% p.a. with a portfolio in the total US market and 1 company I picked for myself on my own analysis. The logic was that if 99% fail to beat the index, but you could pick 1 company that might out perform, you would arguably be doing better than nearly everyone on the globe. I was right so far, 10 years investing between 22 and 32 and I just hit $1m AUD. My goal is $5m, which I should be able to hit with contributions of 50-100k in the next 10 years.

62453341
>>62453307
Well done. Great to see a good noncrybaby post of a winner

62453398
>>62453341
>winner
Not until I have $5m sitting in bonds forever and can quit this capitalist hell but yeah feels good so far

62453406
>>62453307

62453410
The stock market is the only thing propping up people's wealth today. Also Boomers rely on it for their retirement and the govt already blatantly said that Social Security is fucked by 2032 so there's no way they'll let it crash without printing a fuck ton of money to prop it back up again

62453422
>>62453410
Got it, you know everything about now and the future. Why are you so bitter and poor?

62453442
>>62451639
>half of the decades the S&P 500 yields ZERO return.

So? It also has insane bullruns out of sync with fundamentals. You're supposed to hold these funds for 50 years so the bull and bear cycles cancel out. You'll come out very rich as a result, most likely. You can give that wealth to your kids so they can build a future for their kids and so on. This is the one easy way a poorshit can escape generational poverty, if they're patient, if their shit genes can resist taking the cookie out of the jar.

Granted, if you don't have kids, you don't care about long-term wealth anyway. You just care about now, now, now, now, now, cum, cum, cum, cum, cum. So go gamble and get tattoos or whatever. No one cares.
